title: Agostino Di Duccio.
content:
The chime of silver bells; the sweet
    Wild rush of fairy wings and feet;
    The fluting birds of dawn; the small
    And crystal music of the waterfall.
    Or piping of some lone and hidden faun;
    All this you were, and suddenly were not
    A moment's Ariel, centuries forgot!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Margaret Steele Anderson, titled "Agostino Di Duccio.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
